note: Plays before the title card — an animated Holmes silhouette portrait that holds on stage as the house settles. The chosen title card follows on the same row; eight more title-card candidates are further down under "Title card — other ideas".

title: Opening — Holmes portrait (smoking pipe)
source: AI-generated · 1920×1080 · 24fps · ~31.9s · seamless loop (first frame = last frame, exact)
notes: Ornate gilt-framed Holmes silhouette smoking a pipe on a dark ground. Re-cut into a true forward loop: a single smoke puff (~11.9s) billows out from the clean frame, peaks, and fully dissipates, then holds clean for 20s before repeating. The generator's dead ~18s mid-clip hold was removed and the puff that wrapped across the original start/end was stitched continuous; the first and last frames are byte-for-byte identical (verified) so it loops with no reset. Encoded lossless. The generator's corner watermark was removed.

title: Title card — Seedance 2.0 (chosen, looped for show)
source: Seedance 2.0 · 1920×1080 · 24fps · 10.1s base, looped to ~2:09 for the show
notes: The pick — show version, extended to hold on stage. Made with the Seedance 2.0 generator from the black-on-white "animate the text 'The Reluctant Resurrection of Sherlock Holmes'" prompt. The cursive title hand-writes on once over the first ~8.7s, then holds for ~2 minutes: a tiny calm window of the drifting mist ping-pongs so the atmosphere keeps moving without the bigger smoke billow repeating. Audio plays once (music + the pen scribble during the write-on) and the final note is held and faded out gently, then silence. The original 10s base render is kept at clips/tc_seedance2_bw.mp4.

title: Scene 1 — Holmes entrance / exit
notes: Holmes faces front, turns to profile, then walks frame-right and exits. Plain single-subject silhouette.

title: Scene 2 — Moriarty menacing (I)
notes: Moriarty stands menacing at the table, then walks frame-left and exits. Plain single-subject silhouette.

title: Scene 3 — Moriarty menacing (II)
notes: Moriarty enters frame-left, walks to centre, holds centre menacing/gesturing, then walks frame-right. Plain single-subject silhouette. Walk-in/out leg-gap timing de-popped so the legs separate on the same frame as the source instead of snapping open a couple frames late.

title: Scene 4 — Séance: Moriarty flickers to the Ghost
notes: Runway-flicker version, full: real moving Moriarty HARD-CUTS into the runway flicker transition (its glow/grain look kept), which HARD-CUTS into the real Ghost footage for the hood-off and playout. Both seams are size/position-matched and the soft floor is carried across all three parts, so it reads as one continuous shot.

title: Scene 5 — The Falls: fight + falling deerstalker
notes: Trimmed: opens on Moriarty solo, Holmes enters, the grapple at the falls, the deerstalker falls and lands, ending on the fallen deerstalker. Single union silhouette.

title: Scene 6 — Moriarty reaches for Holmes
notes: Moriarty stands center and reaches his right arm toward off-frame Holmes. Plain single-subject silhouette.

note: Eight more title-card candidates, each from a different AI video generator (named in its Source line, taken from the original filename). All share the same design — the cursive title hand-writing on beside a Holmes silhouette with drifting watercolor mist — so they differ mainly in the generator, the resolution/sharpness, and how fast the lettering draws on. Two prompt wordings were used: a "black on white — animate the text" prompt and a "title card for a theater production of" prompt.

title: Title card — Kling O3 4K (black-on-white prompt)
source: Kling O3 4K · 3836×2160 · 10.0s · 24fps · prompt: black-on-white "animate the text"
notes: The only true-4K renders are this and the next one — much sharper than the others. Made with the Kling O3 4K generator from the black-on-white "animate the text" prompt.

title: Title card — Kling O3 4K (theater-title-card prompt)
source: Kling O3 4K · 3836×2160 · 10.0s · 24fps · prompt: "title card for a theater production of"
notes: Same 4K Kling O3 generator as above, but from the "title card for a theater production of" prompt. Same design; the end frame looks near-identical to the other 4K version.

title: Title card — Kling 3.0 Pro (black-on-white prompt)
source: Kling 3.0 Pro · 1916×1080 · 10.0s · 24fps · prompt: black-on-white "animate the text"
notes: Made with Kling 3.0 Pro. Slower write-on: the lettering is still being drawn through most of the clip and finishes near the end, rather than completing early and holding.

title: Title card — Kling 3.0 Pro (theater-title-card prompt)
source: Kling 3.0 Pro · 1916×1080 · 10.0s · 24fps · prompt: "title card for a theater production of"
notes: Kling 3.0 Pro from the theater-title-card prompt. Slowest write-on of the set — the title finishes drawing right at the very end (at the 7-second mark it's a full line behind the black-on-white Kling 3.0 Pro version).

title: Title card — Seedance 2.0 (black-on-white, take 2)
notes: A second Seedance 2.0 take from the same prompt as the chosen one, at 720p. Writes on and then holds; visually very close to the chosen version but a distinct render.

title: Title card — Seedance 2.0 Fast (black-on-white)
source: Seedance 2.0 Fast · 1280×720 · 10.0s · 24fps · prompt: black-on-white "animate the text"
notes: Made with the faster Seedance 2.0 Fast model tier ("Fast" refers to the generator, not the on-screen animation), at 720p. Same design as the chosen Seedance version.

title: Title card — Gen-4.5 (theater-title-card prompt)
source: Gen-4.5 · 1280×720 · 10.0s · 24fps · prompt: "title card for a theater production of"
notes: Made with the Gen-4.5 generator from the theater-title-card prompt, at 720p. Writes on and then holds.

title: Title card — Gen-4.5 (5-second short cut)
source: Gen-4.5 · 1280×720 · 5.0s · 24fps · prompt: "title card for a theater production of"
notes: The short one — same Gen-4.5 generator and prompt as above, but half the length (121 frames vs 241), so the whole write-on plays in about 5 seconds.

note: Filmed before we did it with a scene read. Some are pretty good and we may want to use them. Each is shown twice: the raw footage (for the performance) and its silhouette version. All cut from IMG_2158.MOV. Silhouette versions are pending projectionist review.

notes: Silhouette of the raw clip above. He walks frame-right to frame-left and exits; legs read as an open/merged shadow through the stride.

notes: Silhouette of the raw clip above.

note: Alternatives explored for the Moriarty->Ghost transition (p.76). V10 (full moving front + unified floor) is the chosen Scene 4 above. Kept here for reference: V10t same with a trimmed ~5s front; V9 pre-floor-fix; V8 no moving front; V7 runway with a fade; V1-V3 flicker-out then pop; V4-V6 accelerating flicker.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V10t: floor unified + trimmed front (~5s)
notes: Same as V10 but the moving-Moriarty front is trimmed from ~9s to ~5s (opens with him already moving). Pick whichever front length you like — easy to set anywhere between.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V7: “Runway flicker” (fade version)
notes: First runway version: runway transition (look kept) DISSOLVES into the real Ghost footage. Superseded by V8, which aligns the figures and hard-cuts instead. Kept for comparison.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V1: “Dying bulb”
notes: He blinks fully OUT and back 3 times, the gaps widening each time, then he's gone — a short empty-stage beat — then the Ghost SNAPS in solid. Cleanest read; no ghost peek-through.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V2: “Two worlds”
notes: Same flicker-outs, but in the last gaps the Ghost shows THROUGH instead of white — the two figures phase man↔ghost, one last Moriarty flash 'fighting', then gone and the Ghost pops. Most dramatic; a touch busier.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V3: “Slow collapse”
notes: Only 2 long, slow flicker-outs and a longer fade to gone, then the Ghost MATERIALIZES (one quick blink) and holds. Most deliberate / séance-y.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V4: “Accelerando”
notes: The flicker accelerates smoothly — fast, then much faster — until he snaps out; a short empty beat, then the Ghost hard-pops in solid.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V5: “Two gears”
notes: Two distinct speeds: a few fast flickers, then a much-faster burst, then gone and the Ghost pops. Same idea as V4 but in two clear 'gears' instead of a smooth ramp.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V6: “Charge + materialize”
notes: Moriarty accelerates out (like V4), then the energy passes to the Ghost — she flickers IN fast and settles solid, rather than a single hard pop.
